import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        req = urllib.request.Request(
            page_url, 
            headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SocialMediaScraper/1.0)'}
        )
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Security Fix: Validate the URL scheme to prevent SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery)
        # by ensuring only whitelisted protocols (http/https) are used.
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme. Only http and https are allowed.")

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import ipaddress
import socket


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate URL to prevent SSRF
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if not parsed_url.scheme or not parsed_url.netloc:
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL")

        hostname = parsed_url.hostname
        
        # Resolve hostname to IP address
        try:
            ip_str = socket.gethostbyname(hostname)
        except socket.gaierror:
            raise ValueError("Could not resolve hostname")

        ip = ipaddress.ip_address(ip_str)

        # Define private/reserved IP ranges to block
        private_ranges = [
            ipaddress.ip_network('0.0.0.0/8'),
            ipaddress.ip_network('10.0.0.0/8'),
            ipaddress.ip_network('127.0.0.0/8'),
            ipaddress.ip_network('169.254.0.0/16'),
            ipaddress.ip_network('172.16.0.0/12'),
            ipaddress.ip_network('192.168.0.0/16'),
            ipaddress.ip_network('::1/128'),
            ipaddress.ip_network('fc00::/7'),
            ipaddress.ip_network('fe80::/10'),
        ]

        # Check if IP is in any private/reserved range
        for network in private_ranges:
            if ip in network:
                raise ValueError("Access to private/reserved IP addresses is forbidden")

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched, parsed, or is blocked
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
import re
import certifi
from typing import Dict

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches a webpage and extracts its title and description meta tag.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public webpage.

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page title and description.
        
    Raises:
        ValueError: If the URL is invalid or the page cannot be fetched.
    """
    if not page_url or not isinstance(page_url, str):
        raise ValueError("A valid URL string must be provided.")

    # Ensure the URL has a scheme (http or https)
    if not page_url.startswith(('http://', 'https://')):
        page_url = 'https://' + page_url

    try:
        # Use certifi to provide a reliable CA bundle for SSL verification
        cafile = certifi.where()
        
        # Create a request with a User-Agent to avoid being blocked by some servers
        headers = {
            'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36'
        }
        req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers=headers)
        
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, cafile=cafile) as response:
            # Read and decode the response
            charset = response.headers.get_content_charset()
            if charset is None:
                charset = 'utf-8'
            html_content = response.read().decode(charset)

        # Extract title
        # Regex to match <title> tag, ignoring case and handling whitespace
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', html_content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract description from meta tag
        # Matches <meta name="description" content="..."> or property="og:description"
        description_match = re.search(
            r'<meta\s+[^>]*(?:name|property)\s*=\s*["\'](?:description|og:description)["\'][^>]*content\s*=\s*["\']([^"\']*)["\'][^>]*>|'
            r'<meta\s+[^>]*content\s*=\s*["\']([^"\']*)["\'][^>]*(?:name|property)\s*=\s*["\'](?:description|og:description)["\'][^>]*>',
            html_content,
            re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        
        description = "No description found"
        if description_match:
            # The regex has two capturing groups for content, depending on attribute order
            description = (description_match.group(1) or description_match.group(2) or "").strip()

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        raise ValueError(f"HTTP Error occurred: {e.code} {e.reason}") from e
    except urllib.error.URLError as e:
        raise ValueError(f"URL Error occurred: {e.reason}") from e
    except Exception as e:
        raise ValueError(f"An unexpected error occurred: {str(e)}") from e

import ssl

from html.parser import HTMLParser
from typing import Dict, Optional

class MetadataParser(HTMLParser):
    """
    A lightweight HTML parser to extract title and meta description 
    without relying on external dependencies like BeautifulSoup.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title: Optional[str] = None
        self.description: Optional[str] = None
        self._in_title: bool = False
        self._title_content: list = []

    def handle_starttag(self, tag: str, attrs: list):
        if tag == "title":
            self._in_title = True
        elif tag == "meta":
            attrs_dict = dict(attrs)
            # Check for standard meta description
            name = attrs_dict.get("name", "").lower()
            if name == "description":
                self.description = attrs_dict.get("content")
            # Check for Open Graph description (common in social media)
            property_attr = attrs_dict.get("property", "").lower()
            if property_attr == "og:description":
                self.description = attrs_dict.get("content")

    def handle_endtag(self, tag: str):
        if tag == "title" and self._in_title:
            self._in_title = False
            self.title = "".join(self._title_content).strip()
            self._title_content = []

    def handle_data(self, data: str):
        if self._in_title:
            self._title_content.append(data)

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the HTML content of a given URL and extracts the title 
    and description metadata.
    
    Args:
        page_url (str): The URL of the webpage to scrape.
        
    Returns:
        dict: A dictionary containing 'title' and 'description' keys.
              Values are None if not found.
    """
    result = {
        "title": None,
        "description": None
    }

    # Create SSL context using certifi for secure certificate verification
    context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())

    # Set a standard User-Agent to prevent blocking by some servers
    headers = {
        "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SocialMediaScraper/1.0)"
    }
    req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers=headers)

    try:
        # Use context parameter for secure connection
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context) as response:
            # Read the response body
            html_content = response.read().decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')
            
            # Parse the HTML to extract metadata
            parser = MetadataParser()
            parser.feed(html_content)
            
            result["title"] = parser.title
            result["description"] = parser.description

    except urllib.error.URLError as e:
        # Handle network errors (e.g., connection refused, timeout)
        print(f"URL Error fetching {page_url}: {e.reason}")
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        # Handle HTTP errors (e.g., 404, 500)
        print(f"HTTP Error {e.code} fetching {page_url}: {e.reason}")
    except Exception as e:
        # Handle other unexpected errors
        print(f"Unexpected error fetching {page_url}: {str(e)}")

    return result

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate URL scheme to prevent unintended protocol handling
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            raise ValueError("Only HTTP and HTTPS protocols are supported")

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        req = urllib.request.Request(
            page_url, 
            headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SocialMediaScraper/1.0)'}
        )
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title (handles multiline and ignores case)
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description (handles multiline, ignores case, accounts for single/double quotes)
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
            content, 
            re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import ipaddress


def is_ip_private(ip_str: str) -> bool:
    """
    Check if the given IP string is a private/reserved address.
    """
    try:
        ip = ipaddress.ip_address(ip_str)
        # Check for private IPv4 ranges (RFC 1918) and IPv6 Unique Local Addresses (RFC 4193)
        return ip.is_private or ip.is_loopback or ip.is_reserved or ip.is_link_local
    except ValueError:
        # If the string is not a valid IP address, it's not a private IP
        return False


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate URL scheme
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme")

        # Resolve hostname to IP to check for private addresses
        hostname = parsed_url.hostname
        if not hostname:
            raise ValueError("No hostname found in URL")

        # Get IP address
        try:
            # socket.getaddrinfo returns a list of tuples, we need the first one's address
            # Note: We use socket here to resolve the IP
            import socket
            ip_info = socket.getaddrinfo(hostname, None, proto=socket.IPPROTO_TCP)
            # ip_info[0][4][0] contains the IP address string
            ip_address = ip_info[0][4][0]
        except socket.gaierror:
            raise ValueError("Could not resolve hostname")

        # Security Check: Ensure the IP is not private/local
        if is_ip_private(ip_address):
            raise ValueError("Access to private IP addresses is forbidden")

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError, socket.error):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import re
import ssl
import certifi

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description from a given webpage URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public webpage.

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page title and description.
        Example: {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
        
    Raises:
        ValueError: If the URL is invalid or the page cannot be fetched.
    """
    # Use certifi to provide a reliable set of root certificates for SSL verification
    context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
    
    try:
        # Create the request with a User-Agent header to prevent blocking by some servers
        req = urllib.request.Request(
            page_url, 
            headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SocialMediaScraper/1.0)'}
        )
        
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context) as response:
            # Read the response body, decoding it as UTF-8
            html_content = response.read().decode('utf-8')
            
    except urllib.error.URLError as e:
        raise ValueError(f"Failed to fetch URL: {page_url}. Reason: {e.reason}")
    except Exception as e:
        raise ValueError(f"An error occurred while fetching the page: {str(e)}")

    # Extract title: <title>...</title>
    title_match = re.search(r'<title[^>]*>(.*?)</title>', html_content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
    title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

    # Extract description: <meta name="description" content="...">
    # Handles both single and double quotes for the content attribute
    desc_match = re.search(
        r'<meta\s+[^>]*name\s*=\s*["\']description["\'][^>]*content\s*=\s*["\']([^"\']*)["\'][^>]*>', 
        html_content, 
        re.IGNORECASE
    )
    
    # If the standard description meta tag isn't found, look for Open Graph description
    if not desc_match:
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta\s+[^>]*property\s*=\s*["\']og:description["\'][^>]*content\s*=\s*["\']([^"\']*)["\'][^>]*>', 
            html_content, 
            re.IGNORECASE
        )
        
    description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description found"

    return {'title': title, 'description': description}

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
import ssl
import certifi
from html.parser import HTMLParser
from typing import Dict, Optional

class MetadataParser(HTMLParser):
    """
    A simple HTML parser to extract title and meta description.
    It avoids external dependencies like BeautifulSoup to ensure security and minimal footprint.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title: Optional[str] = None
        self.description: Optional[str] = None
        self._in_title = False
        self._current_tag = None
        self._meta_attrs = None

    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
        attrs_dict = dict(attrs)
        
        if tag == 'title':
            self._in_title = True
        elif tag == 'meta':
            name = attrs_dict.get('name', '').lower()
            property_val = attrs_dict.get('property', '').lower()
            content = attrs_dict.get('content', '')
            
            # Check for standard description meta tag
            if name == 'description' and content:
                self.description = content
            # Check for Open Graph description
            elif property_val == 'og:description' and content:
                self.description = content

    def handle_data(self, data):
        if self._in_title:
            # Clean up whitespace but keep the structure
            clean_data = data.strip()
            if clean_data:
                self.title = clean_data
                self._in_title = False

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the HTML content of a given URL and extracts the title and description.
    
    Args:
        page_url (str): The URL of the webpage to scrape.
        
    Returns:
        dict: A dictionary containing 'title' and 'description' keys. 
              Values are None if not found or if an error occurs.
    """
    # Initialize result with None values
    result = {
        'title': None,
        'description': None
    }

    if not page_url:
        return result

    # Create SSL context using certifi for secure requests
    try:
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
    except Exception:
        # Fallback to default context if certifi fails
        context = ssl.create_default_context()

    # Configure request headers to mimic a browser and prevent 403 errors
    headers = {
        'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SocialMediaScraper/1.0)'
    }
    
    req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers=headers)

    try:
        # Open the URL with the secure context and timeout
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Read the response body
            content_bytes = response.read()
            
            # Attempt to decode the content. Default to utf-8, fallback to latin-1
            try:
                content = content_bytes.decode('utf-8')
            except UnicodeDecodeError:
                content = content_bytes.decode('latin-1')
            
            # Parse the HTML content
            parser = MetadataParser()
            parser.feed(content)
            
            # Populate result
            if parser.title:
                result['title'] = parser.title
            if parser.description:
                result['description'] = parser.description

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, TimeoutError, ValueError):
        # Catch network errors, HTTP errors, timeouts, and invalid URLs
        # Return the default empty result to avoid leaking internal error details
        pass
    except Exception:
        # Catch-all for any other unexpected errors
        pass

    return result

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import html


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Create an SSL context using certifi for trusted root certificates
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL with a timeout to prevent hanging
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        # Using non-greedy matching and handling potential multiline tags
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = html.unescape(title_match.group(1).strip()) if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        # Regex improved to handle attributes in any order (name/content or content/name)
        # and flexible whitespace
        desc_pattern = r'<meta[^>]*?(?:name|property)=["\']description["\'][^>]*?(?:content)=["\'](.*?)["\']|<meta[^>]*?(?:content)=["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*?(?:name|property)=["\']description["\']'
        desc_match = re.search(desc_pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        
        description = "No description available"
        if desc_match:
            # The regex has two capturing groups depending on attribute order.
            # Group 1 is content when name comes first, Group 2 is content when content comes first.
            raw_desc = desc_match.group(1) if desc_match.group(1) else desc_match.group(2)
            if raw_desc:
                description = html.unescape(raw_desc.strip())

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, TimeoutError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        # In a production environment, logging the specific exception is recommended.
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import ipaddress


def is_private_ip(hostname: str) -> bool:
    """
    Checks if the resolved hostname resolves to a private, reserved, or loopback IP address.
    """
    try:
        # Attempt to resolve the hostname to an IP address
        ip = ipaddress.ip_address(hostname)
        # Check if the IP is private, loopback, or reserved
        if ip.is_private or ip.is_loopback or ip.is_reserved:
            return True
    except ValueError:
        # If the hostname is not a valid IP, check if it is a domain
        # We still treat it as potentially unsafe if it resolves to internal addresses
        # but we cannot determine that without a DNS lookup. 
        # For strict SSRF prevention, we block ambiguous hostnames if needed, 
        # but here we rely on the IP check for resolved addresses.
        pass
    return False


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # 1. Validate URL Scheme and Host to prevent SSRF
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        
        # Only allow http and https schemes
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme. Only HTTP and HTTPS are allowed.")
        
        # Get the hostname (handles IPv6 addresses enclosed in brackets)
        hostname = parsed_url.hostname
        if not hostname:
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL: No hostname found.")

        # Check if the hostname resolves to a private IP address
        if is_private_ip(hostname):
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL: Access to private or loopback addresses is forbidden.")

        # 2. Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        # Log the error for debugging if necessary
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
import re
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the HTML content of a given URL and extracts the title and description.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public webpage.

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page title and description.
        Example: {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}

    Raises:
        urllib.error.URLError: If the URL is invalid or the server is unreachable.
    """
    # Set up the request with SSL certificate verification using certifi
    # This ensures secure connections to HTTPS sites
    # Note: urllib.request.create_https_context is not available in standard library
    # We'll use certifi directly with urllib's default SSL context
    import ssl
    ssl_context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
    
    req = urllib.request.Request(
        page_url, 
        headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SocialMediaScraper/1.0)'}
    )

    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ssl_context) as response:
            html_content = response.read().decode('utf-8')
            
            # Extract title using regex
            # Matches <title>...</title> or <title property="...">...</title>
            title_match = re.search(r'<title[^>]*>(.*?)</title>', html_content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
            title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"
            
            # Extract description from meta tag
            # Matches <meta name="description" content="..."> or <meta property="og:description" content="...">
            description_match = re.search(
                r'<meta\s+[^>]*(?:name|property)\s*=\s*["\'](?:description|og:description)["\'][^>]*content\s*=\s*["\']([^"\']*)["\'][^>]*>', 
                html_content, 
                re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
            )
            description = description_match.group(1).strip() if description_match else "No description found"
            
            return {
                'title': title,
                'description': description
            }
            
    except urllib.error.URLError as e:
        # Re-raise the URL error to be handled by the caller
        raise e
    except Exception as e:
        # Handle other potential errors (e.g., decoding issues)
        raise urllib.error.URLError(f"Error processing page: {str(e)}")

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from html.parser import HTMLParser
from typing import Dict, Any
import ssl
import certifi


class MetadataParser(HTMLParser):
    """
    A robust HTML parser to extract title and meta description.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title = None
        self.description = None
        self.in_title = False
        self.current_tag = None
        self.current_attrs = {}

    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
        self.current_tag = tag
        self.current_attrs = dict(attrs)
        
        if tag == 'title':
            self.in_title = True
        
        if tag == 'meta':
            name = self.current_attrs.get('name', '').lower()
            property_val = self.current_attrs.get('property', '').lower()
            content = self.current_attrs.get('content', '')
            
            # Check for standard description meta tag
            if name == 'description' and content:
                self.description = content
            # Check for Open Graph description
            elif property_val == 'og:description' and content:
                self.description = content

    def handle_data(self, data):
        if self.in_title and self.title is None:
            # Clean up whitespace in title
            self.title = data.strip()

    def handle_endtag(self, tag):
        if tag == 'title':
            self.in_title = False


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description.
    """
    try:
        # Create a custom SSL context that allows older TLS versions (1.0, 1.1, 1.2)
        # while still verifying certificates using certifi.
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        context.set_ciphers('DEFAULT@SECLEVEL=1') # Lower security level for compatibility
        context.minimum_version = ssl.TLSVersion.TLSv1 # Allow TLS 1.0

        # Add a User-Agent header to mimic a browser and avoid blocking
        headers = {
            'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36'
        }
        req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers=headers)

        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context) as response:
            # Read and decode content
            content_bytes = response.read()
            try:
                content = content_bytes.decode('utf-8')
            except UnicodeDecodeError:
                # Fallback to latin-1 if utf-8 fails
                content = content_bytes.decode('latin-1')

        # Parse the HTML content
        parser = MetadataParser()
        parser.feed(content)

        # Fallback values if parsing fails to find data
        title = parser.title if parser.title else "No title found"
        description = parser.description if parser.description else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, ssl.SSLError) as e:
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        # Log the error for debugging purposes if needed
        # print(f"Error fetching {page_url}: {e}") 
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }
    except Exception:
        # Catch-all for unexpected errors
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Security Fix: Validate the URL scheme to prevent SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery)
        # Only allow http and https protocols to prevent access to internal services (file://, gopher://, etc.)
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme. Only HTTP and HTTPS are allowed.")

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
import re
import certifi
import ssl

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the HTML content of a webpage and extracts the title and description.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public webpage.

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page title and description.
        Returns empty strings for title/description if they cannot be found.

    Raises:
        urllib.error.URLError: If the URL is invalid or the request fails.
    """
    # Create a context that uses certifi's certificate bundle for SSL verification
    context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())

    # Set a user-agent to mimic a standard browser request
    headers = {
        'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36'
    }

    req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers=headers)

    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context) as response:
            # Read and decode the response using the detected encoding
            # response.read() returns bytes, decode to string
            html_content = response.read().decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')

            # Initialize default values
            title = ""
            description = ""

            # 1. Extract Title
            # Look for <title> tag specifically
            title_match = re.search(r'<title[^>]*>(.*?)</title>', html_content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
            if title_match:
                title = title_match.group(1).strip()
            else:
                # Fallback: Look for Open Graph or Twitter title tags
                og_title_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*property=["\']og:title["\'][^>]*content=["\']([^"\']*)["\']', html_content, re.IGNORECASE)
                if og_title_match:
                    title = og_title_match.group(1).strip()

            # 2. Extract Description
            # Look for meta description tag
            meta_desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\']([^"\']*)["\']', html_content, re.IGNORECASE)
            if meta_desc_match:
                description = meta_desc_match.group(1).strip()
            else:
                # Fallback: Look for Open Graph description
                og_desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*property=["\']og:description["\'][^>]*content=["\']([^"\']*)["\']', html_content, re.IGNORECASE)
                if og_desc_match:
                    description = og_desc_match.group(1).strip()
                else:

                    twitter_desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']twitter:description["\'][^>]*content=["\']([^"\']*)["\']', html_content, re.IGNORECASE)
                    if twitter_desc_match:
                        description = twitter_desc_match.group(1).strip()

            return {'title': title, 'description': description}

    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        # Re-raise HTTP errors (e.g., 404, 500) to be handled by the caller

    except urllib.error.URLError as e:

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
import ssl
import certifi
from html.parser import HTMLParser
from typing import Dict, Optional

class MetadataParser(HTMLParser):
    """
    A lightweight HTML parser to extract title and meta description 
    without relying on external dependencies like BeautifulSoup.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title: Optional[str] = None
        self.description: Optional[str] = None
        self._in_title: bool = False
        self._title_content: list = []

    def handle_starttag(self, tag: str, attrs: list[tuple[str, Optional[str]]]) -> None:
        if tag == "title":
            self._in_title = True
        
        if tag == "meta":
            attr_dict = dict(attrs)
            name = attr_dict.get("name", "").lower()
            property_attr = attr_dict.get("property", "").lower()
            content = attr_dict.get("content")

            # Check for standard meta description
            if name == "description" and content:
                self.description = content
            
            # Check for Open Graph description (common in social media)
            elif property_attr == "og:description" and content:
                self.description = content

    def handle_data(self, data: str) -> None:
        if self._in_title:
            self._title_content.append(data)

    def handle_endtag(self, tag: str) -> None:
        if tag == "title":
            self._in_title = False
            if self._title_content:
                self.title = "".join(self._title_content).strip()

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches a webpage and extracts title and description metadata.
    
    Args:
        page_url: The URL of the webpage to scrape.
        
    Returns:
        A dictionary containing 'title' and 'description' keys.
        Returns empty strings for fields if they cannot be determined.
        
    Raises:
        urllib.error.URLError: If the URL is invalid or the request fails.
    """
    # Validate URL scheme to prevent potential SSRF or unsafe requests
    if not page_url.startswith(('http://', 'https://')):
        raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme. Only HTTP and HTTPS are supported.")

    # Create SSL context using certifi for secure certificate verification
    context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())

    req = urllib.request.Request(
        page_url, 
        headers={'User-Agent': 'SocialMediaScraper/1.0'}  # Identify the bot
    )

    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context) as response:
            # Read the response body
            content_bytes = response.read()
            
            # Attempt to decode the content safely
            content_type = response.headers.get_content_charset()
            if not content_type:
                content_type = 'utf-8'
            
            try:
                html_content = content_bytes.decode(content_type, errors='ignore')
            except (UnicodeDecodeError, LookupError):
                # Fallback to utf-8 if charset detection fails
                html_content = content_bytes.decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')

            # Parse the HTML
            parser = MetadataParser()
            parser.feed(html_content)

            return {
                "title": parser.title or "",
                "description": parser.description or ""
            }

    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        # Handle HTTP errors (4xx, 5xx)
        raise urllib.error.URLError(f"HTTP Error {e.code}: {e.reason}") from e
    except urllib.error.URLError:
        # Re-raise URL errors (e.g., connection issues)
        raise

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate URL scheme to prevent potential SSRF or local file access
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            raise ValueError("Only HTTP and HTTPS protocols are allowed")

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0'})
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme. Only HTTP and HTTPS are allowed.")

        hostname = parsed_url.hostname

        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        # Log the error 'e' here if needed for debugging
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

    A simple HTML parser to extract the title and meta description.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title: Optional[str] = None
        self.description: Optional[str] = None
        self.in_title_tag: bool = False
        self.current_tag: str = ""
        self.current_attrs: Dict[str, str] = {}

    def handle_starttag(self, tag: str, attrs: list[tuple[str, Optional[str]]]):
        self.current_tag = tag
        self.current_attrs = dict(attrs)
        
        if tag == "title":
            self.in_title_tag = True
        elif tag == "meta":
            name = self.current_attrs.get("name", "").lower()
            if name == "description":
                self.description = self.current_attrs.get("content", "")

    def handle_data(self, data: str):
        if self.in_title_tag:
            if self.title is None:
                self.title = data.strip()
            else:
                self.title += data.strip()

    def handle_endtag(self, tag: str):
        if tag == "title":
            self.in_title_tag = False

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the HTML content of a given URL and extracts the title and description.
    
    Args:
        page_url (str): The URL of the webpage to scrape.
        
    Returns:
        dict: A dictionary containing 'title', 'description', and 'url' keys.
              If an error occurs, returns a dictionary with an 'error' key.
    """
    # Initialize the result dictionary
    result = {
        "url": page_url,
        "title": None,
        "description": None
    }

    try:
        # 1. Create the SSL Context using certifi for secure HTTPS requests
        # This prevents MITM attacks by verifying certificates against the CA bundle provided by certifi.
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())

        # 2. Create the request with a User-Agent header
        # Some servers block requests without a User-Agent.
        req = urllib.request.Request(
            page_url, 
            headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SocialMediaScraper/1.0)'}
        )

        # 3. Open the URL using the secure context
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Read the response body
            raw_html = response.read()
            
            # 4. Parse the HTML content
            parser = MetadataParser()
            # Decode using the detected encoding (usually utf-8)
            try:
                charset = response.headers.get_content_charset()
                if charset:
                    html_content = raw_html.decode(charset)
                else:
                    # Fallback to utf-8 with error ignoring
                    html_content = raw_html.decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')
            except Exception:
                # Ultimate fallback for decoding
                html_content = raw_html.decode('latin-1', errors='ignore')

            parser.feed(html_content)

            # Update result with parsed data
            result["title"] = parser.title
            result["description"] = parser.description

    except urllib.error.URLError as e:
        result["error"] = f"URL Error: {str(e.reason)}"
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        result["error"] = f"HTTP Error: {e.code} {e.reason}"
    except Exception as e:
        result["error"] = f"Unexpected error: {str(e)}"

    return result

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Create an SSL context using certifi for secure connections
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        
        # Add a User-Agent header to avoid being blocked by servers
        headers = {
            'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36'
        }
        
        req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers=headers)
        
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        # Updated regex to be more robust: allows attributes in any order and handles optional self-closing slashes
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
            content, 
            re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        # Fallback: check if content comes before name (less common but possible)
        if not desc_match:
            desc_match = re.search(
                r'<meta[^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*name=["\']description["\']',
                content, 
                re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
            )
            
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError):

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Security Fix: Validate URL scheme to prevent SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery)
        # Only allow http and https schemes to prevent access to internal networks (e.g., file://, gopher://, etc.)
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            return {
                'title': 'Invalid URL scheme',
                'description': 'Only HTTP and HTTPS URLs are allowed'
            }

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from html.parser import HTMLParser
import ssl

class MetadataHTMLParser(HTMLParser):
    """
    A simple HTML parser to extract the title and meta description 
    from an HTML document.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title = ""
        self.description = ""
        self.in_title = False
        self.in_meta_description = False

    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
        attrs_dict = dict(attrs)
        
        if tag == "title":
            self.in_title = True
        elif tag == "meta":
            name = attrs_dict.get("name", "").lower()
            property_val = attrs_dict.get("property", "").lower()
            content = attrs_dict.get("content", "")
            
            # Check for standard meta description
            if name == "description" and content:
                self.description = content
            
            # Check for Open Graph description
            elif property_val == "og:description" and content:
                self.description = content

    def handle_endtag(self, tag):
        if tag == "title":
            self.in_title = False

    def handle_data(self, data):
        if self.in_title:
            self.title = data.strip()

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the HTML content of a given URL and extracts the title 
    and description using Python's built-in libraries.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public webpage.

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page title and description.
        Returns empty strings for fields if they cannot be found.
    """
    metadata = {
        "title": "",
        "description": ""
    }

    # Create an SSL context that uses certifi for certificate verification
    # This ensures secure HTTPS requests using the provided dependency
    try:
        import certifi
        ssl_context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
    except ImportError:
        # Fallback to default context if certifi is not available
        ssl_context = ssl.create_default_context()

    try:
        # Set a user agent to mimic a browser, as some servers block default Python requests
        headers = {
            "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SocialMediaScraper/1.0)"
        }
        req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers=headers)
        
        # Open the URL with the custom SSL context
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ssl_context) as response:
            content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type', '')
            
            # Ensure we are parsing text-based content (HTML)
            if 'text/html' not in content_type:
                return metadata

            # Read and decode the response
            raw_html = response.read().decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')

            # Parse the HTML to extract metadata
            parser = MetadataHTMLParser()
            parser.feed(raw_html)
            
            metadata["title"] = parser.title
            metadata["description"] = parser.description

    except urllib.error.URLError as e:
        # Handle network errors or invalid URLs gracefully
        print(f"Error fetching URL {page_url}: {e}")
    except Exception as e:
        # Handle other potential errors (e.g., parsing errors)
        print(f"An unexpected error occurred: {e}")

    return metadata

        self.description: Optional[str] = None
        self._in_title: bool = False
        self._current_title_data: list = []

    def handle_starttag(self, tag: str, attrs: list):
        if tag == "title":
            self._in_title = True
            self._current_title_data = []
        elif tag == "meta":
            attrs_dict = dict(attrs)
            name = attrs_dict.get("name", "").lower()
            property_attr = attrs_dict.get("property", "").lower()
            content = attrs_dict.get("content", "")

            # Check for standard description or Open Graph description
            if (name == "description" or property_attr == "og:description") and content:
                # Prefer standard description, but accept og:description if standard is missing
                if self.description is None:
                    self.description = content

    def handle_data(self, data: str):
        if self._in_title:
            self._current_title_data.append(data)

    def handle_endtag(self, tag: str):
        if tag == "title" and self._in_title:
            self.title = "".join(self._current_title_data)
            self._in_title = False


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches a webpage and extracts title and description metadata.

    Args:
        page_url (str): The URL of the webpage to scrape.

    Returns:
        dict: A dictionary containing 'title' and 'description' keys.
              Values are None if not found or on error.
    """
    metadata = {
        "title": None,
        "description": None
    }

    if not page_url:
        return metadata

    # Create a secure SSL context using certifi
    context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())

    # Set a user-agent to mimic a browser and avoid 403 Forbidden errors on some sites
    headers = {
        "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SocialMediaScraper/1.0)"
    }

    try:
        req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers=headers)
        
        # Open the URL with the secure context
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type', '')
            
            # Ensure we are parsing text-based content
            if 'text/html' in content_type:
                html_content = response.read().decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')
                
                parser = MetadataParser()
                parser.feed(html_content)
                
                metadata["title"] = parser.title
                metadata["description"] = parser.description

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, ValueError, TimeoutError):
        # Catch network errors, HTTP errors (4xx, 5xx), URL format errors, and timeouts.
        # Return empty/default metadata as per the requirement to handle errors gracefully.
        pass

    return metadata

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse, quote
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # SECURITY FIX: Validate URL scheme to prevent SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery)
        # Only allow http and https schemes. Reject file://, ftp://, etc.
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            return {
                'title': 'Security Restriction',
                'description': 'Invalid or unsafe URL scheme detected.'
            }

        # SECURITY FIX: Quote the URL to handle unsafe characters and ensure proper encoding
        safe_url = quote(page_url, safe='/:?&=@#')

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(safe_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        # Improved regex to be more flexible with attribute order and whitespace
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from html.parser import HTMLParser
import ssl
import certifi

class MetadataParser(HTMLParser):
    """
    A simple HTML parser to extract title and meta description tags.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title = ""
        self.description = ""
        self.in_title = False
        self.in_meta = False
        self.meta_attrs = {}
        self.title_complete = False

    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
        if tag == "title" and not self.title_complete:
            self.in_title = True
        elif tag == "meta":
            self.in_meta = True
            self.meta_attrs = dict(attrs)
            self._process_meta_tag()
            self.in_meta = False

    def handle_endtag(self, tag):
        if tag == "title":
            self.in_title = False
            self.title_complete = True

    def handle_data(self, data):
        if self.in_title:
            self.title += data.strip()

    def _process_meta_tag(self):
        # Check for standard description meta tag
        name = self.meta_attrs.get("name", "").lower()
        if name == "description":
            content = self.meta_attrs.get("content", "")
            if content:
                self.description = content.strip()
        
        # Check for Open Graph description (og:description)
        property_attr = self.meta_attrs.get("property", "").lower()
        if property_attr == "og:description":
            content = self.meta_attrs.get("content", "")
            if content and not self.description: # Prefer standard meta if exists
                self.description = content.strip()

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches a webpage and extracts the title and description metadata.
    
    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public webpage.
        
    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page title and description.
        Returns empty strings for fields if they cannot be found.
        
    Raises:
        urllib.error.URLError: If the URL is invalid or unreachable.
    """
    # Create an SSL context using certifi for secure connections
    context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
    
    # Set a user agent to prevent blocking by some servers
    headers = {
        'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; MetadataFetcher/1.0)'
    }
    
    req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers=headers)
    
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context) as response:
            # Read the response, limiting to a reasonable size (e.g., 1MB) 
            # to prevent memory issues with large files
            raw_data = response.read(1024 * 1024) 
            
            # Attempt to decode the response
            # Check for charset in headers, default to utf-8
            content_type = response.headers.get_content_charset()
            if not content_type:
                content_type = 'utf-8'
            
            try:
                html_content = raw_data.decode(content_type, errors='ignore')
            except (UnicodeDecodeError, LookupError):
                # Fallback to utf-8 with ignore if specific charset fails
                html_content = raw_data.decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')

            # Parse the HTML content
            parser = MetadataParser()
            parser.feed(html_content)
            
            return {
                'title': parser.title,
                'description': parser.description
            }
            
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        raise urllib.error.URLError(f"HTTP Error {e.code}: {e.reason}") from e
    except urllib.error.URLError:
        # Re-raise URL errors (e.g., invalid scheme, network issues)
        raise

class MetadataParser(html.parser.HTMLParser):
    """
    A simple HTML parser to extract title and meta description.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title: Optional[str] = None
        self.description: Optional[str] = None
        self.in_title: bool = False
        self.title_buffer: list = []
        
        # Track current tag and attributes for meta parsing
        self.current_tag: str = ""
        self.current_attrs: Dict[str, str] = {}

    def handle_starttag(self, tag: str, attrs: list[tuple[str, Optional[str]]]) -> None:
        self.current_tag = tag.lower()
        self.current_attrs = {k.lower(): v for k, v in attrs if v is not None}
        
        if tag.lower() == "title":
            self.in_title = True
            self.title_buffer = []
        elif tag.lower() == "meta":
            name = self.current_attrs.get("name", "").lower()
            if name == "description":
                self.description = self.current_attrs.get("content", "")

    def handle_data(self, data: str) -> None:
        if self.in_title:
            self.title_buffer.append(data)

    def handle_endtag(self, tag: str) -> None:
        if tag.lower() == "title" and self.in_title:
            self.in_title = False
            self.title = "".join(self.title_buffer).strip()

    }

        # Open the URL with the secure context

            html_content_bytes = response.read()
            
            # Decode the content. We try UTF-8 first, which is standard.
            # If it fails, we fall back to latin-1 or ignore errors to prevent crashing.
            try:
                html_content = html_content_bytes.decode('utf-8')
            except UnicodeDecodeError:
                html_content = html_content_bytes.decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')

            # Parse the HTML

            # Update result with extracted data
            result["title"] = parser.title

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, ValueError) as e:

        pass

        pass

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0'})
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import ipaddress


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Parse the URL to extract the hostname
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if not parsed_url.netloc:
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL format")

        # Resolve the hostname to an IP address
        import socket
        hostname = parsed_url.hostname
        try:
            ip_str = socket.gethostbyname(hostname)
        except socket.gaierror:
            raise urllib.error.URLError(f"Could not resolve hostname: {hostname}")

        # Validate the IP address to prevent SSRF (Block private/reserved IPs)
        ip = ipaddress.ip_address(ip_str)
        if ip.is_private or ip.is_loopback or ip.is_reserved or ip.is_link_local:
            raise urllib.error.URLError("Access to internal or reserved addresses is forbidden")

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import re
import ssl

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches a webpage and extracts its title and description metadata.
    
    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public webpage.
        
    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page title and description.
        
    Raises:
        ValueError: If the URL is invalid or empty.
        urllib.error.URLError: If the page cannot be fetched.
    """
    if not page_url or not isinstance(page_url, str):
        raise ValueError("page_url must be a non-empty string")
    
    # Create SSL context using certifi for secure connections
    ssl_context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
    
    try:
        # Create request with a user-agent header to avoid being blocked
        request = urllib.request.Request(
            page_url,
            headers={
                'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36'
            }
        )
        
        # Open the URL with SSL context
        with urllib.request.urlopen(request, context=ssl_context) as response:
            html_content = response.read().decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')
            
        # Extract title using regex
        title_match = re.search(r'<title[^>]*>(.*?)</title>', html_content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else None
        
        # Extract description from meta tag
        # Look for <meta name="description" content="...">
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta\s+[^>]*name\s*=\s*["\']description["\'][^>]*content\s*=\s*["\']([^"\']*)["\'][^>]*>',
            html_content,
            re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        
        # If not found, try property="og:description"
        if not desc_match:
            desc_match = re.search(
                r'<meta\s+[^>]*property\s*=\s*["\']og:description["\'][^>]*content\s*=\s*["\']([^"\']*)["\'][^>]*>',
                html_content,
                re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
            )
        
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else None
        
        # Return the metadata dictionary
        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }
        
    except urllib.error.URLError as e:
        raise urllib.error.URLError(f"Failed to fetch URL '{page_url}': {e}")
    except Exception as e:
        raise Exception(f"An error occurred while processing the page: {e}")
